title: Why a Big Car Park Company Closed Down
summary: A big car park company in the UK called National Car Parks (NCP) has collapsed, putting many jobs at risk. People were surprised because it charged high prices for parking. The company had many car parks in places like airports and town centres. But fewer people are using them now because more people work from home and shop online instead of going to stores. This means less money for NCP. Also, costs like energy and rent went up a lot, making it hard for the company to pay its bills. Some people even prefer to risk a fine rather than pay the high parking fees. So, NCP couldn't make enough profit and had to close.

keywords.explanation: when a company stops working and can't pay its bills
people might lose their work because the company is closing
people doing their jobs from their houses instead of going to an office
buying things on the internet instead of going to stores
the money a company has to spend to run its business
a penalty you pay for breaking a rule, like parking without a ticket

questions.question: What happened to National Car Parks (NCP)?
Why were people surprised about NCP's collapse?
Where did NCP have car parks?
Why are fewer people using NCP's car parks now?
options: More people work from home and shop online
The car parks are too small
NCP closed all its car parks
questions.correct_answer: More people work from home and shop online

questions.question: What made it hard for NCP to pay its bills?
options: Costs like energy and rent went up
It moved to a new country
questions.correct_answer: Costs like energy and rent went up

questions.question: What do some people do instead of paying NCP's high fees?

background_read: National Car Parks (NCP) was a big company in the UK that owned many parking lots where people could leave their cars. Companies like this make money by charging fees for parking. But when fewer people need to park, like when they work from home or shop online, the company earns less. Also, if costs like electricity and rent get too high, it can be hard to keep the business running. This is what happened to NCP, leading to its collapse and putting jobs in danger.
Article_Structure: WHO: National Car Parks (NCP), a big car park company in the UK
WHAT: It collapsed, putting jobs at risk because it couldn't make enough profit
WHERE: It had car parks in places like airports, train stations, hospitals, and town centres across the UK
WHY: Fewer people used its car parks due to more working from home and online shopping, and costs like energy and rent went up

perspectives.description: NCP couldn't adapt to changes like more people working from home and rising costs, leading to its collapse
